description: Starter deterministic chat-quality cases. These use mock frames for dry-run mode; replace channel_id and sources with real local channel data for context/live runs.

cases.question: What does he say a brand should promise?
cases.notes: Starter specific-answer case with a source-registry frame and one valid [S1] citation.

sources.title: Brand Strategy Basics
sources.quote: A brand is a promise backed by repeated values and consistent behavior.
sources.text: A brand is a promise backed by repeated values and consistent behavior. If the audience cannot predict the value, the promise is not real yet.

mock_frames.text: He frames a brand as a promise that has to be backed by repeated values and consistent behavior [S1].

cases.question: How does he explain retention loops?
cases.notes: Uses two sources and ensures source recall only requires at least one expected cited video.

sources.title: Audience Retention
sources.quote: Retention is a feedback loop: deliver a useful moment, then give people a reason to repeat the behavior.
sources.text: Retention is a feedback loop: deliver a useful moment, then give people a reason to repeat the behavior. The repeat action matters more than a one-time spike.

sources.title: Growth Notes
sources.quote: A reminder or next step can turn attention into a second session.
sources.text: A reminder or next step can turn attention into a second session. That second session is the earliest sign the loop is working.

mock_frames.text: He describes retention as a feedback loop where a useful moment creates a reason to repeat the behavior [S1].

cases.question: What does he say about quantum pricing rituals?
cases.notes: Unsupported question should produce an evidence refusal instead of invented claims.

mock_frames.text: There is not enough caption evidence in the source pack to answer that.

cases.question: What operating principle does he give for launches?
cases.notes: Exercises backend source-registry frame shape and [S1]-style citation parsing.
